docs.title: RavenRustRAG
docs.text: RavenRustRAG is a local-first RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) engine written in Rust. It provides millisecond document indexing, microsecond query latency, and ships as a single static binary with zero runtime dependencies.

docs.title: What is RAG?
docs.text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation combines document retrieval with LLM prompting. Instead of feeding an entire corpus to a language model, RAG retrieves the most relevant chunks and provides them as context. This reduces hallucinations, keeps responses grounded in your data, and works within token limits.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Vector search with cosine similarity over local embeddings
  • HNSW index for O(log n) approximate nearest neighbor search
  • Hybrid search combining BM25 keyword matching with vector retrieval (RRF fusion)
  • Knowledge graph for entity-relationship traversal and graph-vector fusion
  • Semantic splitting via embedding cosine similarity between sentences
  • Lock-free caching with DashMap for concurrent embedding cache access
  • Memory-mapped SQLite with 256 MB mmap for zero-copy reads
  • HTTP API (Axum) with Bearer auth, rate limiting, CORS, and OpenAPI schema
  • MCP server for AI assistant integration (Claude, Copilot, Cursor)
  • CLI with 13 commands for scripting and automation
  • File watching with debounce for automatic re-indexing
  • Incremental indexing via SHA-256 content fingerprinting
  • Docker support with minimal scratch-based images (~15 MB)

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
RavenRustRAG is a Cargo workspace with 9 crates:

Crate Purpose raven-core Document, Chunk, SearchResult, Config, RavenError, cosine similarity raven-embed Embedder trait + Ollama/OpenAI/Dummy backends + DashMap cache raven-store VectorStore trait + SQLite (WAL, mmap) / Memory / HNSW stores raven-split Splitter trait + Text/Sentence/Token/Semantic splitters raven-load File loaders (txt, md, csv, json, html, pdf, docx) + JSONL export raven-search DocumentIndex, BM25, KnowledgeGraph, Reranker, MultiQuery, Eval raven-server Axum HTTP API (auth, CORS, rate limit, metrics, OpenAPI) raven-mcp MCP server (stdio JSON-RPC, 4 tools) raven-cli CLI binary (13 commands)

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: See Installation to get the binary, then Quick Start to index your first documents.

docs.title: HTTP API
docs.text: RavenRustRAG includes an HTTP API server built with Axum.

docs.title: Starting the Server
docs.text:
raven serve --port 8484

With authentication:

RAVEN_API_KEY=my-secret-key raven serve

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
When RAVEN_API_KEY is set, protected endpoints require a Bearer token:

Authorization: Bearer my-secret-key

Endpoints that never require auth: /health, /ready, /openapi.json

Endpoints that require auth by default (configurable via public_stats = true in config): /stats, /metrics, /collections

Endpoints that always require auth: /query, /prompt, /index, /documents

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Health check. Always returns 200.

{"status": "ok"}

docs.title: GET /ready
docs.text: Readiness check. Returns 200 when the server is ready to accept queries.

docs.title: GET /stats
docs.text:
Index statistics.

{
  "total_chunks": 1523,
  "total_documents": 42,
  "database_size_bytes": 8388608
}

docs.title: GET /metrics
docs.text: Prometheus-format metrics (request counts, latencies).

docs.title: GET /openapi.json
docs.text: OpenAPI 3.0 schema for the API.

docs.title: POST /query
docs.text:
Search documents. Requires auth (when configured).

Request: 

{
  "query": "how does authentication work",
  "top_k": 5,
  "hybrid": false,
  "alpha": 0.5,
  "filter": {
    "source": "docs/auth.md"
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "results": [
    {
      "text": "Authentication is handled via Bearer tokens...",
      "score": 0.892,
      "metadata": {
        "source": "docs/auth.md",
        "doc_id": "abc123"
      }
    }
  ],
  "query_time_ms": 12
}

docs.title: POST /prompt
docs.text:
Search and format as an LLM prompt. Requires auth.

Request: 

{
  "query": "explain the build process",
  "top_k": 3
}

Response: 

{
  "prompt": "Use the following context to answer the question.\n\nContext:\n[1] (score: 0.89) ...\n\nQuestion: explain the build process",
  "sources": ["docs/build.md"],
  "query_time_ms": 15
}

docs.title: POST /index
docs.text:
Add documents to the index. Requires auth.

Request: 

{
  "documents": [
    {
      "content": "Document text content here",
      "metadata": {
        "source": "manual-entry",
        "title": "My Doc"
      }
    }
  ]
}

Response: 

{
  "indexed": 1,
  "chunks": 3
}

docs.title: DELETE /documents
docs.text:
Delete documents by source path. Requires auth.

Request: 

{
  "source": "docs/old-file.md"
}

docs.title: GET /collections
docs.text: List available collections (when multi-collection is enabled).

docs.title: CORS
docs.text: The server includes permissive CORS headers by default, allowing requests from any origin. This is suitable for development; in production, configure a reverse proxy with stricter policies.

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text: The server applies token-bucket rate limiting to prevent abuse. Default: 100 requests per second (configurable via rate_limit_per_second in config).

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: RavenRustRAG is a Cargo workspace with 9 crates organized in a layered dependency hierarchy.

docs.title: Crate Overview
docs.text:
raven-core  (no internal deps)
  ↑
raven-embed (depends on raven-core)
raven-split (depends on raven-core)
raven-load  (depends on raven-core)
raven-store (depends on raven-core)
  ↑
raven-search (depends on core, embed, store, split)
  ↑
raven-server (depends on core, embed, store, split, search)
raven-mcp    (depends on core, search, split)
  ↑
raven-cli    (depends on all)

docs.title: raven-core
docs.text:
Foundation types shared by all crates:

  • Document — input document with content and metadata
  • Chunk — text segment with embedding vector and metadata
  • SearchResult — ranked result with score and source info
  • Config — TOML-based configuration
  • RavenError — unified error type using thiserror
  • fingerprint() — content hashing for incremental indexing
  • cosine_similarity() — optimized vector comparison

docs.title: raven-embed
docs.text:
Embedding abstraction layer:

  • Embedder trait — async interface for text-to-vector conversion
  • OllamaEmbedder — local inference via Ollama HTTP API
  • OpenAIEmbedder — cloud inference via OpenAI API
  • DummyEmbedder — deterministic fake embeddings for testing
  • EmbeddingCache — lock-free DashMap cache with LRU-style eviction
  • CachedEmbedder — transparent caching wrapper for any Embedder

docs.title: raven-split
docs.text:
Text chunking strategies:

  • Splitter trait — async interface for text splitting
  • TextSplitter — character-based with configurable overlap
  • SentenceSplitter — respects sentence boundaries
  • TokenSplitter — approximate token-based splitting
  • SemanticSplitter — groups sentences by embedding similarity

docs.title: raven-load
docs.text:
Document ingestion:

  • Loader — detects format by extension and loads documents
  • Format handlers: text, markdown (with frontmatter), CSV, JSON, JSONL, HTML
  • export_jsonl() / import_jsonl() — backup and restore
  • Directory traversal with extension filtering

docs.title: raven-store
docs.text:
Vector storage backends:

  • VectorStore trait — async CRUD + similarity search
  • SqliteStore — persistent storage with WAL, mmap, cosine similarity
  • MemoryStore — in-memory store for testing
  • HnswIndex — approximate nearest neighbor index
  • MetadataFilter — key-value filtering on search results
  • Fingerprint tracking for incremental indexing

docs.title: raven-search
docs.text:
Pipeline orchestrator:

  • DocumentIndex — builder-pattern coordinator (split → embed → store → search)
  • BM25Index — keyword search with TF-IDF scoring
  • KnowledgeGraph / GraphRetriever — entity extraction and graph traversal
  • MultiQueryExpander — query expansion via keyword extraction
  • Reranker trait + KeywordReranker — result re-scoring
  • EvalMetrics — MRR, NDCG, Precision@k, Recall@k
  • Watch mode with debounce for live re-indexing

docs.title: raven-server
docs.text:
HTTP API:

  • Axum-based server with Tower middleware
  • Bearer token authentication
  • CORS configuration
  • Rate limiting
  • OpenAPI 3.0 schema generation
  • Prometheus metrics endpoint
  • Structured JSON responses

docs.title: raven-mcp
docs.text:
AI assistant integration:

  • MCP (Model Context Protocol) server over stdio
  • JSON-RPC 2.0 message handling
  • Tool definitions: search, get_prompt, collection_info, index_documents
  • Input validation and error reporting

docs.title: raven-cli
docs.text:
User-facing binary:

  • Clap-derived command parser
  • All commands: index, query, prompt, serve, watch, graph, info, clear, export, import, mcp, doctor, benchmark
  • Structured and JSON output modes
  • Config file loading

docs.title: Design Principles
docs.text:
1. Thread-safe by default: All public types are Send + Sync
  2. No unwrap in library code: Proper error propagation with ?
  3. Batch operations: Embedding and storage work in batches for throughput
  4. Zero-copy where possible: mmap for SQLite, &str parameters
  5. Builder pattern: Complex types use builders (DocumentIndex)
  6. Trait-based abstraction: All major components are behind traits for testability
  7. Lock-free concurrency: DashMap + atomics where possible, Mutex only when necessary

docs.title: Indexing Pipeline
docs.text:
Files → Loader → Documents → Splitter → Chunks → Embedder → Vectors
                                                                  ↓
                                                            VectorStore (SQLite)
                                                                  ↓
                                                            BM25 terms stored

docs.title: Query Pipeline
docs.text:
Query → Embedder → Query Vector → VectorStore.search() → Results
                                                              ↓
                  (optional) → BM25.search() → RRF fusion → Reranker → Final Results

docs.title: Knowledge Graph
docs.text:
Chunks → Entity Extraction → Graph Nodes + Edges → JSON file
                                                        ↓
Query → Entity Extraction → Graph Traversal → Related Chunks → Fusion with vector results

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to RavenRustRAG are documented here.

This project follows Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• DOCX file loader support (ZIP-based XML extraction, behind docx feature flag)
  • PDF file loader support (pdf-extract, behind pdf feature flag)
  • raven ask command for local LLM question-answering via Ollama (#63)
  • Generator trait and OllamaGenerator for LLM text generation with streaming
  • raven completions command for shell completion generation (bash, zsh, fish, elvish, PowerShell) (#62)
  • raven status command for rich index health dashboard (#74)
  • --dry-run mode for raven index (#71)
  • Colored CLI output with term highlighting (#73)
  • Schema migration system with versioned upgrades for SqliteStore (#60)
  • Property-based tests with proptest for core, split, and search crates (#58)
  • Stress tests for concurrent indexing and large document handling (#59)
  • Fuzz targets for text splitter, all loaders, and cosine similarity (#57)
  • mdBook + MkDocs documentation site
  • HNSW integration in SqliteStore for O(log n) vector search (#64)
  • VectorStore::get_by_doc_id() for efficient parent-child retrieval (#65)
  • Split read/write connections in SqliteStore for concurrent reads (#69)
  • LRU cache eviction via moka crate (#68)
  • Auto-detect embedding dimension from model (#67)
  • Full raven.toml config wiring in CLI (#66)
  • DashMap lock-free embedding cache (#47)
  • Memory-mapped I/O for SQLite (256 MB mmap) (#48)
  • CI concurrency groups to prevent queue flooding (#46)
  • Knowledge graph CLI commands (raven graph build, raven graph query) (#45)
  • Multi-target release binaries (linux-amd64, linux-musl, linux-arm64, darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64) (#54)
  • GitHub Actions CI with fmt, clippy, test, MSRV, bench stages
  • Docker workflow with GHCR publishing
  • CodeQL security scanning

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Default --extensions for CLI index/watch commands now includes all supported formats (txt,md,csv,json,html,pdf,docx)
  • EmbeddingCache internals: Mutex replaced with DashMap + AtomicU64
  • SqliteStore uses separate read/write connections for better concurrency
  • Semaphore error in batch embedding now returns proper RavenError instead of panic

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• SqliteStore search now uses HNSW for O(log n) instead of O(n) brute-force (#64)
  • query_parent() no longer loads entire database (#65)
  • Embedding dimension no longer hardcoded to 768 (#67)
  • CLI now reads and applies raven.toml configuration (#66)
  • Clippy compliance: removed unwrap in library code

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Cargo workspace with 9 crates
  • Core types: Document, Chunk, SearchResult, Config, RavenError
  • Embedding backends: Ollama, OpenAI, DummyEmbedder
  • Embedding cache with configurable max size
  • CachedEmbedder wrapper
  • Text splitters: TextSplitter, SentenceSplitter, TokenSplitter, SemanticSplitter
  • File loaders: txt, md (with frontmatter), csv, json, jsonl, html
  • JSONL export/import
  • Vector stores: SqliteStore (WAL, persistent), MemoryStore (testing)
  • HNSW approximate nearest neighbor index
  • Metadata filtering on search results
  • DocumentIndex builder pattern pipeline orchestrator
  • BM25 keyword search with persistent term storage
  • Hybrid search with RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion)
  • Knowledge graph: entity extraction, graph building, traversal, graph-vector fusion
  • Multi-query expansion via keyword extraction
  • Reranker trait with KeywordReranker
  • Evaluation metrics: MRR, NDCG, Precision@k, Recall@k
  • Watch mode with debounce for live re-indexing
  • Multi-collection router
  • Parent-child retrieval
  • Axum HTTP server with auth, CORS, rate limiting, OpenAPI schema, metrics
  • MCP server (stdio JSON-RPC) with search, prompt, info, index tools
  • CLI: index, query, prompt, serve, watch, graph, info, clear, export, import, mcp, doctor, benchmark
  • Incremental indexing via content fingerprinting
  • Criterion benchmarks
  • Pre-commit hooks (fmt, clippy, test)
  • Docker multi-stage build (Alpine builder, scratch runtime)

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text: Flag Short Default Description --config -c — Path to config file --verbose -v false Enable verbose logging --json — false Output as JSON (for scripting) --log-format — text Log format: text or json (env: RAVEN_LOG_FORMAT)

docs.title: raven index <path>
docs.text:
Index documents from a directory or file.

raven index ./documents/ --extensions txt,md,html --chunk-size 1024
Flag Short Default Description --db -d ./raven.db Database path --backend -b ollama Embedding backend (ollama or openai) --url -u http://localhost:11434 Ollama URL --model -m nomic-embed-text Embedding model --chunk-size — 512 Chunk size in characters --chunk-overlap — 50 Overlap between chunks --extensions — txt,md File extensions to include (comma-separated)

docs.title: raven query <query>
docs.text:
Search the index.

raven query "how does authentication work" --top-k 10 --hybrid
Flag Short Default Description --db -d ./raven.db Database path --backend -b ollama Embedding backend --url -u http://localhost:11434 Ollama URL --model -m nomic-embed-text Embedding model --top-k -k 5 Number of results --hybrid — false Use hybrid BM25 + vector search with RRF --alpha — 0.5 Hybrid blend (1.0 = pure vector, 0.0 = pure BM25)

docs.title: raven prompt <query>
docs.text:
Generate an LLM-ready prompt with retrieved context.

raven prompt "explain the architecture" --top-k 5
Flag Short Default Description --db -d ./raven.db Database path --backend -b ollama Embedding backend --url -u http://localhost:11434 Ollama URL --model -m nomic-embed-text Embedding model --top-k -k 3 Number of context chunks

docs.title: raven serve
docs.text:
Start the HTTP API server.

RAVEN_API_KEY=secret raven serve --port 8484
Flag Short Default Env Var Description --host — 127.0.0.1 RAVEN_HOST Bind address --port -p 8484 RAVEN_PORT Port --db -d ./raven.db — Database path --backend -b ollama — Embedding backend --url -u http://localhost:11434 — Ollama URL --model -m nomic-embed-text — Embedding model --api-key — — RAVEN_API_KEY API key for auth (optional)

docs.title: raven watch <path>
docs.text:
Watch a directory and auto-index on file changes.

raven watch ./notes/ --debounce 1000 --extensions md,txt
Flag Short Default Description --db -d ./raven.db Database path --backend -b ollama Embedding backend --url -u http://localhost:11434 Ollama URL --model -m nomic-embed-text Embedding model --extensions — txt,md File extensions to watch --debounce — 500 Debounce interval in milliseconds

docs.title: raven graph <subcommand>
docs.text: Build or query the knowledge graph.

docs.title: raven graph build
docs.text:
raven graph build --output ./my-graph.json
Flag Short Default Description --db -d ./raven.db Database path --output -o ./raven-graph.json Graph output file

docs.title: raven graph query <query>
docs.text:
raven graph query "Rust async patterns" --max-hops 3
Flag Short Default Description --graph -g ./raven-graph.json Graph file path --max-hops — 2 Max traversal hops --top-k -k 5 Number of results

docs.title: raven info
docs.text:
Show index statistics (document count, chunk count, database size).

raven info --db ./raven.db

docs.title: raven clear
docs.text:
Clear all indexed data.

raven clear --db ./raven.db

docs.title: raven export
docs.text:
Export the index to JSONL format for backup.

raven export --output backup.jsonl
Flag Short Default Description --output -o raven-export.jsonl Output file path --db -d ./raven.db Database path

docs.title: raven import <file>
docs.text:
Import documents from a JSONL file.

raven import backup.jsonl
Flag Short Default Description --db -d ./raven.db Database path --backend -b ollama Embedding backend --url -u http://localhost:11434 Ollama URL --model -m nomic-embed-text Embedding model

docs.title: raven mcp
docs.text:
Start the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server on stdio for AI assistant integration.

raven mcp --db ./raven.db
Flag Short Default Description --db -d ./raven.db Database path --backend -b ollama Embedding backend --url -u http://localhost:11434 Ollama URL --model -m nomic-embed-text Embedding model

docs.title: raven doctor
docs.text:
Run system diagnostics (Ollama connectivity, model availability, database health).

raven doctor
Flag Short Default Description --url -u http://localhost:11434 Ollama URL to check --db -d ./raven.db Database path to check

docs.title: raven benchmark
docs.text:
Run performance benchmarks.

raven benchmark --num-docs 500 --iterations 100
Flag Short Default Description --num-docs -n 100 Number of documents to generate --iterations -i 50 Number of query iterations

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: RavenRustRAG can be configured via a TOML config file, environment variables, or CLI flags.

docs.title: Config File
docs.text:
Create a raven.toml in the project root or pass --config <path>:

[embedder]
backend = "ollama"
model = "nomic-embed-text"
url = "http://localhost:11434"

[splitter]
kind = "text"
chunk_size = 512
chunk_overlap = 50

[pipeline]
embed_batch_size = 64
store_batch_size = 100

[server]
host = "127.0.0.1"
port = 8484
# api_key = "your-secret"
# cors_origins = ["http://localhost:3000"]
request_timeout_secs = 60
rate_limit_per_second = 100
max_query_length = 10000
public_stats = false

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Purpose Default RAVEN_API_KEY API authentication key (server) — (no auth) RAVEN_DB Default database path ./raven.db RAVEN_MODEL Default embedding model nomic-embed-text RAVEN_HOST Server bind address 127.0.0.1 RAVEN_PORT Server port 8484 RAVEN_LOG_FORMAT Log output format (text or json) text

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text: CLI flags > Environment variables > Config file > Defaults

docs.title: Ollama (default)
docs.text:
Local inference via Ollama. Requires Ollama to be running with a pulled model.

OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1 ollama serve

Recommended models: - nomic-embed-text (768 dimensions, fast, good quality) - mxbai-embed-large (1024 dimensions, higher quality)

docs.title: OpenAI
docs.text:
Uses the OpenAI embeddings API. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable.

raven index ./docs --backend openai --model text-embedding-3-small

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
RavenRustRAG uses SQLite as its vector store with these optimizations enabled by default:

  • WAL mode for concurrent read access
  • mmap (256 MB) for zero-copy reads
  • 64 MB page cache
  • 5 second busy timeout for write contention

The database file is portable and can be copied between machines with the same architecture.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions to RavenRustRAG are welcome. This guide covers the development workflow.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Rust 1.86+ (MSRV)
  • Ollama (for integration tests with real embeddings)
  • Git

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/egkristi/ravenrustrag.git
cd ravenrustrag
cargo build
cargo test

docs.title: Development Workflow
docs.text:
1. Check GitHub Issues for open work
  2. Create a branch from main
  3. Implement your changes
  4. Run the full check suite:
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings
cargo test --all
  1. Commit with a conventional message
  2. Open a Pull Request

docs.title: Commit Messages
docs.text:
Use conventional commits:

feat: add batch embedding support #12
fix: handle empty document gracefully closes #15
refactor: extract BM25 into separate module
docs: add hybrid search documentation
test: add property tests for splitter
perf: switch to DashMap for cache

docs.title: Code Standards
docs.text:
• Edition: Rust 2021
  • Async runtime: Tokio (full features)
  • Error handling: thiserror for library errors, anyhow only in CLI
  • Traits: Use async-trait; all traits must be Send + Sync
  • Serialization: serde + serde_json
  • Logging: tracing crate (info!, warn!, error! — never println! in libraries)
  • No unwrap() in library code: Use ? and proper error types
  • Tests: Unit tests in each crate, use MemoryStore + DummyEmbedder for isolation

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
ravenrustrag/
├── Cargo.toml          # Workspace root
├── book.toml           # mdBook config
├── mkdocs.yml          # MkDocs config
├── docs/               # Documentation (shared by mkdocs + mdbook)
├── crates/
│   ├── raven-core/     # Foundation types
│   ├── raven-embed/    # Embedding backends
│   ├── raven-split/    # Text chunking
│   ├── raven-load/     # File loading
│   ├── raven-store/    # Vector storage
│   ├── raven-search/   # Pipeline orchestrator
│   ├── raven-server/   # HTTP API
│   ├── raven-mcp/      # MCP server
│   └── raven-cli/      # CLI binary
└── .github/workflows/  # CI/CD

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
Run all tests:

cargo test --all

Run tests for a specific crate:

cargo test -p raven-search

For test isolation, use MemoryStore (in-memory vector store) and DummyEmbedder (deterministic fake embeddings). This avoids needing Ollama running for unit tests.

docs.title: MkDocs (primary, used for GitHub Pages)
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs-material mkdocs-minify-plugin
mkdocs serve     # Local preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000
mkdocs build     # Build to site/

docs.title: mdBook
docs.text:
cargo install mdbook
mdbook serve     # Local preview at http://127.0.0.1:3000
mdbook build     # Build to book/

docs.title: Release Process
docs.text:
Releases are automated via GitHub Actions when a tag is pushed:

git tag v0.1.0
git push origin v0.1.0

This triggers cross-compilation for 5 targets and creates a GitHub Release with binaries and checksums.

docs.title: Docker
docs.text: RavenRustRAG is available as a minimal Docker image based on scratch (no OS, just the binary).

docs.title: Pulling the Image
docs.text:
docker pull ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest

Tags: - latest — latest release from main - vX.Y.Z — specific version - sha-<commit> — specific commit

docs.title: Running
docs.text:
# Basic usage
docker run --rm -v ./data:/data ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest info --db /data/raven.db

# Start server
docker run -d \
  --name ravenrag \
  -p 8484:8484 \
  -v ./data:/data \
  -e RAVEN_API_KEY=my-secret \
  ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest \
  serve --host 0.0.0.0 --db /data/raven.db

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
services:
  ravenrag:
    image: ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest
    ports:
      - "8484:8484"
    volumes:
      - ./data:/data
    environment:
      - RAVEN_API_KEY=${RAVEN_API_KEY}
    command: serve --host 0.0.0.0 --db /data/raven.db
    restart: unless-stopped

  ollama:
    image: ollama/ollama
    ports:
      - "11434:11434"
    volumes:
      - ollama-data:/root/.ollama
    environment:
      - OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1

volumes:
  ollama-data:

docs.title: Building Locally
docs.text:
docker build -t ravenrustrag .

The Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build: 1. Alpine-based Rust builder (musl for static linking) 2. scratch runtime (binary + ca-certificates only)

The resulting image is typically under 20 MB.

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• Runs as non-root user (UID 65534)
  • No shell, no OS utilities (scratch base)
  • TLS certificates bundled for HTTPS outbound connections
  • API key authentication for protected endpoints

docs.title: File Formats
docs.text: RavenRustRAG supports loading documents in several formats. The loader is selected automatically based on file extension.

docs.title: Supported Formats
docs.text: Extension Loader Notes .txt Text Plain text, also used as fallback for unknown extensions .md Markdown YAML frontmatter parsed into metadata .csv CSV Each row becomes a document; headers become metadata keys .json JSON Pretty-printed content for chunking .jsonl JSONL Each line treated as a separate record .html HTML Tags stripped, scripts/styles removed

docs.title: Markdown
docs.text:
Markdown files are loaded with full content preserved. If the file contains YAML frontmatter, it is parsed into document metadata:

---
title: My Document
author: John Doe
tags: [rust, rag]
---

# Content starts here

The frontmatter fields (title, author, tags) become searchable metadata.

docs.title: CSV
docs.text:
Each row in a CSV file becomes a separate document. Column headers are used as metadata keys:

title,content,category
Introduction,Welcome to the guide,basics
Setup,Install with cargo,getting-started

docs.title: JSON
docs.text: JSON files are pretty-printed and treated as a single document for chunking. Nested structures are preserved in the text representation.

docs.title: HTML
docs.text: HTML files have all tags stripped. <script> and <style> blocks are completely removed. The resulting plain text is then chunked normally.

docs.title: Filtering by Extension
docs.text:
When indexing a directory, use --extensions to limit which files are processed:

# Only index markdown and text files
raven index ./docs --extensions md,txt

# Include HTML files
raven index ./web --extensions html,md,txt

The default extensions are txt,md.

docs.title: Hybrid Search
docs.text: RavenRustRAG combines vector similarity search with BM25 keyword matching using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF).

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Vector search: Embeds the query and finds chunks with the highest cosine similarity
  2. BM25 search: Tokenizes the query and scores chunks using TF-IDF term frequency
  3. RRF fusion: Merges both ranked lists using reciprocal rank fusion with configurable alpha blending

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
raven query "rust error handling" --hybrid --alpha 0.7
  • --hybrid enables BM25+vector fusion
  • --alpha controls the blend (1.0 = pure vector, 0.0 = pure BM25, default 0.5)

docs.title: API
docs.text:
POST /query
{
  "query": "rust error handling",
  "top_k": 5,
  "hybrid": true,
  "alpha": 0.7
}

docs.title: When to Use Hybrid Search
docs.text: Scenario Recommendation Short keyword queries Hybrid (BM25 helps with exact matches) Natural language questions Pure vector often sufficient Code/technical terms Hybrid (exact term matching helps) Typos/paraphrases Pure vector (semantic similarity handles variations)

docs.title: BM25 Implementation
docs.text:
The BM25 index is stored alongside vector embeddings in SQLite. Terms are tokenized, lowercased, and stored per chunk. The index is built automatically during document indexing and persisted for fast retrieval.

Parameters (hardcoded defaults): - k1 = 1.2 (term frequency saturation) - b = 0.75 (document length normalization)

docs.title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion
docs.text:
RRF combines rankings without requiring score normalization:

RRF_score(d) = alpha * 1/(k + rank_vector(d)) + (1-alpha) * 1/(k + rank_bm25(d))

Where k = 60 (standard RRF constant).

docs.title: From GitHub Releases (recommended)
docs.text:
Download the latest pre-built binary from GitHub Releases:

# Linux (glibc)
curl -LO https://github.com/egkristi/ravenrustrag/releases/latest/download/raven-linux-amd64
chmod +x raven-linux-amd64 && mv raven-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/raven

# Linux (musl, fully static)
curl -LO https://github.com/egkristi/ravenrustrag/releases/latest/download/raven-linux-amd64-musl
chmod +x raven-linux-amd64-musl && mv raven-linux-amd64-musl /usr/local/bin/raven

# macOS (Apple Silicon)
curl -LO https://github.com/egkristi/ravenrustrag/releases/latest/download/raven-darwin-arm64
chmod +x raven-darwin-arm64 && mv raven-darwin-arm64 /usr/local/bin/raven

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
Requires Rust 1.86+ (MSRV):

git clone https://github.com/egkristi/ravenrustrag.git
cd ravenrustrag
cargo build --release
# Binary at target/release/raven

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
docker pull ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest
docker run --rm -v ./data:/data ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest --help

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
For embedding generation, you need Ollama running locally:

# Install Ollama
curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

# Pull the default embedding model
OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1 ollama pull nomic-embed-text

# Start Ollama (if not running as a service)
OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1 ollama serve

Important: Always run Ollama with OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1 to ensure no cloud inference is used.

docs.title: Knowledge Graph
docs.text: RavenRustRAG can build a knowledge graph from indexed documents to enable entity-relationship traversal alongside vector search.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The knowledge graph extracts entities (proper nouns, technical terms) from chunks and creates edges between entities that co-occur. This enables:

  • Finding related documents through entity connections
  • Multi-hop traversal from query entities to distant but relevant content
  • Graph-vector fusion for enriched search results

docs.title: Building the Graph
docs.text:
# Build from indexed documents
raven graph build --db ./raven.db --output ./raven-graph.json

The graph is serialized as JSON and can be rebuilt at any time from the indexed chunks.

docs.title: Querying the Graph
docs.text:
raven graph query "Rust async runtime" --max-hops 2 --top-k 5

This: 1. Extracts entities from the query 2. Finds matching nodes in the graph 3. Traverses up to max-hops edges 4. Returns chunks associated with discovered entities

docs.title: Entity Extraction
docs.text: Entities are extracted using pattern matching: - Proper nouns: Capitalized words not at sentence start - Technical terms: CamelCase identifiers, acronyms, hyphenated compounds - Multi-word entities: Consecutive capitalized words (e.g., "Knowledge Graph")

docs.title: Graph Structure
docs.text:
{
  "nodes": {
    "Rust": {
      "entity": "Rust",
      "chunk_ids": ["chunk_1", "chunk_5"],
      "frequency": 12
    }
  },
  "edges": [
    {
      "from": "Rust",
      "to": "Tokio",
      "weight": 3
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Graph-Vector Fusion
docs.text: When using the graph retriever programmatically, results can be fused with vector search results for comprehensive retrieval that combines semantic similarity with structural relationships.

docs.title: MCP Server
docs.text: RavenRustRAG includes an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for integration with AI assistants like Claude Desktop.

docs.title: Starting
docs.text:
raven mcp --db ./raven.db

The MCP server communicates over stdio using JSON-RPC 2.0.

docs.title: Configuration for Claude Desktop
docs.text:
Add to your claude_desktop_config.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "ravenrag": {
      "command": "/path/to/raven",
      "args": ["mcp", "--db", "/path/to/raven.db"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Configuration for VS Code (Copilot)
docs.text:
Add to .vscode/mcp.json:

{
  "servers": {
    "ravenrag": {
      "command": "raven",
      "args": ["mcp", "--db", "./raven.db"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: search
docs.text:
Query the index with semantic search.

Parameters: - query (string, required): Search query text - top_k (integer, optional): Number of results (default: 5)

docs.title: get_prompt
docs.text:
Search and format results as an LLM-ready prompt with citations.

Parameters: - query (string, required): Query text - top_k (integer, optional): Number of context chunks (default: 3)

docs.title: collection_info
docs.text:
Get index statistics (document count, chunk count, database size).

No parameters required.

docs.title: index_documents
docs.text:
Add documents to the index.

Parameters: - documents (array, required): Array of { content, metadata } objects

docs.title: Protocol Details
docs.text:
The MCP server implements: - initialize / initialized handshake - tools/list — returns available tools - tools/call — executes a tool - Proper error responses with JSON-RPC error codes

Input validation: - Queries are limited to 10,000 characters - top_k must be between 1 and 100 - Batch indexing is limited to 100 documents per call

docs.title: Migration from Python RavenRAG
docs.text: This guide helps users of the Python RavenRAG (v0.7.0) migrate to RavenRustRAG.

docs.title: Why Migrate?
docs.text: Dimension Python RavenRAG RavenRustRAG Startup time ~2 seconds <10 ms Query latency 50-200 ms 1-5 ms Memory usage 200+ MB 15-30 MB Deployment Python + venv + ChromaDB Single static binary Concurrency GIL-limited Full multi-threaded Type safety Runtime errors Compile-time guarantees Docker image 800+ MB <20 MB

docs.title: Storage Backend
docs.text:
• Python: ChromaDB (separate process or embedded)
  • Rust: SQLite with WAL mode (embedded, no separate process)

There is no ChromaDB support in RavenRustRAG. SQLite is the only persistent backend.

docs.title: Embedding Models
docs.text: Both versions support Ollama with nomic-embed-text as the default. The embedding dimensions and similarity scores are compatible.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Python (YAML): 

embedding:
  provider: ollama
  model: nomic-embed-text
chunking:
  chunk_size: 512
  overlap: 50

Rust (TOML or CLI flags): 

[embedding]
backend = "ollama"
model = "nomic-embed-text"

[pipeline]
chunk_size = 512
chunk_overlap = 50

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text: Python Rust Notes ravenrag index <path> raven index <path> Same ravenrag query <text> raven query <text> Same ravenrag serve raven serve Different default port (8484 vs 8000) ravenrag collections list raven info Simplified — raven watch <path> New: file watching — raven graph build New: knowledge graph — raven mcp New: MCP server — raven doctor New: diagnostics — raven benchmark New: benchmarking

docs.title: API Changes
docs.text:
The HTTP API is largely compatible but with some differences:

Python endpoint Rust endpoint Changes POST /search POST /query Renamed, same body format POST /index POST /index Same GET /health GET /health Same GET /collections GET /stats Different response format

docs.title: Features Not Yet Ported
docs.text:
Some Python features are planned but not yet implemented:

  • ONNX embedding backends (planned)
  • Cross-encoder reranking with ONNX models (planned)
  • Multi-collection routing (basic support exists)
  • Parent-child retrieval (implemented)

docs.title: New Features in Rust
docs.text:
• Hybrid BM25+vector search with RRF fusion
  • Knowledge graph retrieval
  • MCP server for AI assistants
  • File watching with debounce
  • Incremental indexing via fingerprinting
  • Lock-free concurrent cache
  • Memory-mapped SQLite reads
  • Prometheus metrics
  • Built-in benchmarking
  • JSONL export/import

docs.title: 1. Export from Python
docs.text:
If you have indexed data in ChromaDB, export it:

# From Python RavenRAG
ravenrag export --output backup.jsonl

docs.title: 2. Install Rust Version
docs.text:
curl -LO https://github.com/egkristi/ravenrustrag/releases/latest/download/raven-linux-amd64
chmod +x raven-linux-amd64 && mv raven-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/raven

docs.title: 3. Re-index Documents
docs.text:
Since the storage backends are incompatible (ChromaDB vs SQLite), re-index from source:

raven index ./your-documents/ --extensions md,txt,html

docs.title: 4. Verify
docs.text:
raven info
raven query "test query from your domain"
raven doctor

docs.title: 5. Update Integrations
docs.text:
• Update API URL and port (default changed to 8484)
  • Update endpoint names (/search → /query)
  • MCP integration is new — add to Claude/VS Code config

docs.title: Performance Comparison
docs.text:
After migration, run benchmarks to verify improvements:

raven benchmark --num-docs 500 --iterations 100

Typical improvements observed: - 10-50x faster query latency - 5-10x faster indexing throughput - 10x less memory usage - <1% of Docker image size

docs.title: Performance Tuning
docs.text: This guide covers techniques to maximize RavenRustRAG throughput and minimize latency.

docs.title: Embedding Cache
docs.text:
RavenRustRAG uses a lock-free in-memory embedding cache (DashMap) that avoids redundant embedding API calls. The cache is especially effective when:

  • Re-indexing documents with overlapping content
  • Running multiple queries with similar phrasing
  • Using the watch command with incremental updates

Cache stats are available via:

raven info --verbose

docs.title: SQLite Optimizations
docs.text:
The following PRAGMAs are applied automatically:

PRAGMA Value Effect journal_mode WAL Concurrent reads during writes synchronous NORMAL Faster writes (safe with WAL) cache_size -64000 64 MB page cache mmap_size 268435456 256 MB memory-mapped I/O busy_timeout 5000 5s retry on lock contention

docs.title: WAL Mode
docs.text: Write-Ahead Logging allows readers to proceed without blocking on writes. This is critical for the server where queries and indexing happen concurrently.

docs.title: Memory-Mapped I/O
docs.text: With mmap enabled, SQLite reads bypass the userspace buffer and read directly from the OS page cache. This eliminates syscall overhead for hot data and is especially beneficial for large indexes.

docs.title: Batch Sizes
docs.text:
Embedding and storage operations are batched. Tune batch sizes based on your hardware:

  • Embedding batch: Number of texts sent per API call (default varies by backend)
  • Concurrent batches: Controlled by semaphore (default: 4 concurrent embedding calls)

Larger batches reduce API overhead but increase memory usage and latency per batch.

docs.title: Chunk Size Tuning
docs.text:
Chunk Size Pros Cons 256 chars Fine-grained retrieval, precise matches More chunks to store/search, fragmented context 512 chars (default) Good balance of precision and context — 1024 chars More context per result, fewer chunks Less precise matches, may include irrelevant text

For technical documentation, 512 is usually optimal. For narrative content, 1024 may work better.

docs.title: Hybrid Search Performance
docs.text: BM25 search adds minimal overhead since terms are pre-computed during indexing. The RRF fusion step is O(n) in the number of results. For most use cases, hybrid search adds less than 1ms to query time.

docs.title: Hardware Recommendations
docs.text: Component Minimum Recommended RAM 512 MB 2+ GB (for large indexes with mmap) Storage HDD works SSD strongly recommended CPU Any x86_64/arm64 Multi-core for concurrent embedding

docs.title: Benchmarking
docs.text:
Use the built-in benchmark command to measure your system's performance:

raven benchmark --num-docs 1000 --iterations 100

This generates synthetic documents, indexes them, and measures query latency percentiles.

docs.title: Monitoring
docs.text:
The HTTP server exposes /metrics in Prometheus format with:

  • Request count by endpoint
  • Latency histograms
  • Active connections
  • Cache hit/miss rates

docs.title: Index Documents
docs.text:
Point RavenRustRAG at a directory or file to index:

# Index a directory of documents
raven index ./docs/

# Index a single file
raven index README.md

# Index with verbose output
raven index ./notes/ -v

Supported formats: .txt, .md, .csv, .json, .jsonl, .html

docs.title: Query Your Documents
docs.text:
# Simple semantic search
raven query "how does authentication work"

# Get more results
raven query "error handling patterns" --top-k 10

# Hybrid search (BM25 + vector)
raven query "configuration file" --hybrid

docs.title: Generate LLM Prompts
docs.text:
The prompt command wraps search results in an LLM-ready format:

raven prompt "explain the build process"

Output:

Use the following context to answer the question.

Context:
[1] (score: 0.89) The build process uses cargo build...
[2] (score: 0.85) Release builds are created with...

Question: explain the build process

docs.title: Check Index Status
docs.text: raven info

docs.title: Export and Import
docs.text:
# Backup your index to JSONL
raven export > backup.jsonl

# Restore from backup
raven import backup.jsonl

docs.title: Start the HTTP Server
docs.text:
# Start on default port 8484
raven serve

# With API key authentication
RAVEN_API_KEY=my-secret-key raven serve --port 8484

docs.title: Run Diagnostics
docs.text:
raven doctor

This checks Ollama connectivity, embedding model availability, and database health.

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: RavenRustRAG uses a multi-layered testing strategy to ensure correctness and performance.

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# All tests
cargo test --all

# Specific crate
cargo test -p raven-search

# With output
cargo test --all -- --nocapture

# Single test
cargo test -p raven-store test_sqlite_store

docs.title: Unit Tests
docs.text:
Each crate contains unit tests in #[cfg(test)] modules. Tests use:

  • MemoryStore: In-memory vector store that requires no SQLite
  • DummyEmbedder: Deterministic embeddings based on text hash (no Ollama needed)
  • TextSplitter: Simple character-based splitting for predictable chunk boundaries

This ensures tests run fast, in parallel, and without external dependencies.

docs.title: Integration Tests
docs.text:
Tests that exercise the full pipeline (index → query) use DocumentIndex with real store backends:

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_document_index() {
    let store = MemoryStore::new(4);
    let embedder = DummyEmbedder::new(4);
    let splitter = TextSplitter::new(100, 20);

    let index = DocumentIndex::builder()
        .store(Arc::new(store))
        .embedder(Arc::new(embedder))
        .splitter(Arc::new(splitter))
        .build()
        .unwrap();

    let docs = vec![Document::new("Test content here")];
    index.add_documents(&docs).await.unwrap();

    let results = index.query("test", 5).await.unwrap();
    assert!(!results.is_empty());
}

docs.title: Server Tests
docs.text:
The HTTP server tests use axum::test helpers to send requests without starting a real TCP listener:

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_health() {
    let app = create_app(/* ... */);
    let response = app
        .oneshot(Request::builder().uri("/health").body(Body::empty()).unwrap())
        .await
        .unwrap();
    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);
}

docs.title: MCP Tests
docs.text: MCP tests simulate JSON-RPC message exchanges over an in-memory channel, verifying protocol compliance and tool execution.

docs.title: Test Coverage
docs.text:
Run coverage with cargo-tarpaulin:

cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
cargo tarpaulin --all --out html
# Report at tarpaulin-report.html

docs.title: Benchmarks
docs.text:
Performance benchmarks use Criterion:

cargo bench

Benchmarks measure: - Embedding cache lookup (hit/miss) - SQLite insert/query at various scales - Cosine similarity computation - Text splitting throughput - Full pipeline (index + query) end-to-end

Results are tracked in CI via benchmark-action/github-action-benchmark and published to the gh-pages branch.

docs.title: Linting
docs.text:
# Clippy (strict mode for library code)
cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings

# Format check
cargo fmt --all --check

docs.title: Property Testing
docs.text:
For critical algorithms (cosine similarity, text splitting, BM25 scoring), property-based tests using proptest verify invariants:

  • Cosine similarity is always in [-1, 1]
  • Splitting never loses text content
  • BM25 scores are non-negative
  • RRF fusion preserves result count

docs.title: Fuzz Testing
docs.text:
Security-sensitive parsers (JSON-RPC, HTTP request bodies) can be fuzz-tested:

cargo install cargo-fuzz
cargo fuzz run mcp_parse

docs.title: CI Integration
docs.text:
All tests run on every push and PR via GitHub Actions:

  1. cargo fmt --all --check
  2. cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings
  3. cargo test --all
  4. MSRV check (Rust 1.86)
  5. Benchmarks (on main branch pushes)

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common issues and solutions when using RavenRustRAG.

docs.title: "Failed to connect to Ollama"
docs.text:
Symptoms: Indexing or querying fails with a connection error.

Solutions:

  1. Verify Ollama is running: 

    curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags

  2. Start Ollama if not running: 

    OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1 ollama serve

  3. If using a non-default URL, pass --url: 

    raven query "test" --url http://my-host:11434

  4. Run diagnostics: 

    raven doctor

docs.title: "Model not found"
docs.text:
Symptoms: Embedding fails with a model error.

Solutions:

  1. Pull the model: 

    OLLAMA_NO_CLOUD=1 ollama pull nomic-embed-text

  2. Verify it's available: 

    ollama list

  3. If using a different model, specify it: 

    raven index ./docs --model mxbai-embed-large

docs.title: "Database is locked"
docs.text:
Symptoms: Operations fail with SQLite busy/locked errors.

Solutions:

  1. This usually resolves within 5 seconds (busy_timeout). If persistent:
  2. Check for zombie processes holding the lock: 
    fuser raven.db  # Linux
lsof raven.db   # macOS
  3. Remove stale WAL files (only if no process is using the DB): 
    rm raven.db-wal raven.db-shm

docs.title: "Dimension mismatch"
docs.text:
Symptoms: Query fails because the embedding dimension doesn't match stored vectors.

Cause: You indexed with one model (e.g., 768-dim nomic-embed-text) but are querying with a different model (e.g., 1536-dim text-embedding-3-small).

Solution: Use the same model for indexing and querying, or clear and re-index:

raven clear
raven index ./docs --model nomic-embed-text
raven query "test" --model nomic-embed-text

docs.title: "No documents found"
docs.text:
Symptoms: raven index reports 0 documents.

Solutions:

  1. Check file extensions match. Default is txt,md: 

    raven index ./docs --extensions md,txt,html,json

  2. Verify the path contains files: 

    find ./docs -type f \( -name "*.md" -o -name "*.txt" \)

docs.title: "Duplicate indexing / growing database"
docs.text:
Symptoms: Database grows on every re-index even without content changes.

Solution: RavenRustRAG uses fingerprinting for incremental indexing. If your files haven't changed, re-indexing should be a no-op. If the database grows unexpectedly:

  1. Check if file modification times are changing (backups, syncing tools)
  2. Clear and re-index from scratch: 
    raven clear && raven index ./docs

docs.title: "Address already in use"
docs.text:
Symptoms: raven serve fails to bind.

Solutions:

  1. Use a different port: 

    raven serve --port 8485

  2. Find and kill the existing process: 

    lsof -i :8484
kill <PID>

docs.title: "401 Unauthorized"
docs.text:
Symptoms: API calls return 401.

Solution: Include the Bearer token:

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $RAVEN_API_KEY" http://localhost:8484/query \
  -d '{"query": "test"}'

docs.title: Slow Indexing
docs.text:
1. Check Ollama is running on the same machine (network latency)
  2. Consider a faster model (nomic-embed-text is fast; larger models are slower)
  3. Reduce chunk overlap to generate fewer chunks
  4. Use SSD storage for the database

docs.title: Slow Queries
docs.text:
1. Check database size — very large indexes benefit from mmap (enabled by default)
  2. Reduce top_k if you don't need many results
  3. Avoid hybrid search if BM25 isn't needed for your use case
  4. Run raven benchmark to baseline your system

docs.title: "Permission denied" on volume
docs.text:
Symptoms: Container can't write to mounted volume.

Solution: The container runs as UID 65534. Ensure the host directory is writable:

chmod 777 ./data  # or chown to 65534
docker run -v ./data:/data ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest info --db /data/raven.db

docs.title: Container can't reach Ollama
docs.text:
Solution: Use host networking or the host's IP:

# Linux
docker run --network host ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest \
  query "test" --url http://localhost:11434

# macOS/Windows (Docker Desktop)
docker run ghcr.io/egkristi/ravenrustrag:latest \
  query "test" --url http://host.docker.internal:11434

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
1. Run raven doctor for automated diagnostics
  2. Use --verbose flag for detailed logging
  3. Use --json for machine-parseable output
  4. Check GitHub Issues
